contentBlocks.original:
जगद्धात्रि नमस्तुभ्यं विष्णोश्च प्रियवल्लभे ।
यतो ब्रह्मादयो देवाः सृष्टिस्थित्यन्तकारिणः ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagaddhātri namastubhyaṃ viṣṇośca priyavallabhe .
yato brahmādayo devāḥ sṛṣṭisthityantakāriṇaḥ ..1..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to mother of the universe,
Who is the darling wife of Lord Vishnu,
And due to you only, Brahma and others,
Are engaged in creation, upkeep and destruction.
contentBlocks.meanings: जगद्धात्रि (jagaddhātri) – mother of the universe (jagat:universe | dhātri:mother); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; तुभ्यं (tubhyaṃ) – to you; विष्णोः (viṣṇoḥ) – of Vishnu; च (ca) – and; प्रियवल्लभे (priyavallabhe) – darling wife (priya:darling | vallabhe:wife); यतः (yataḥ) – due to whom; ब्रह्मादयः (brahmādayaḥ) – Brahma and others (brahma:Brahma | ādayaḥ:and others); देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; सृष्टिस्थित्यन्तकारिणः (sṛṣṭisthityantakāriṇaḥ) – engaged in creation, upkeep, destruction (sṛṣṭi:creation | sthiti:upkeep | anta:destruction | kāriṇaḥ:engaged in);

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तुलसि कल्याणि नमो विष्णुप्रिये शुभे ।
नमो मोक्षप्रदे देवि नमः सम्पत्प्रदायिके ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastulasi kalyāṇi namo viṣṇupriye śubhe .
namo mokṣaprade devi namaḥ sampatpradāyike ..2..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to the benevolent Tulsi,
Salutations to the holy darling of Vishnu
Salutations to goddess giving salvation,
Salutations to her who grants wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; तुलसि (tulasi) – Tulsi; कल्याणि (kalyāṇi) – benevolent; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; विष्णुप्रिये (viṣṇupriye) – darling of Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | priye:darling); शुभे (śubhe) – holy; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; मोक्षप्रदे (mokṣaprade) – giving salvation (mokṣa:salvation | prade:giving); देवि (devi) – goddess; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; सम्पत्प्रदायिके (sampatpradāyike) – grants wealth (sampat:wealth | pradāyike:grants);

contentBlocks.original:
तुलसी पातु मां नित्यं सर्वापद्भ्योऽपि सर्वदा ।
कीर्तितापि स्मृता वापि पवित्रयति मानवम् ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasī pātu māṃ nityaṃ sarvāpadbhyo'pi sarvadā .
kīrtitāpi smṛtā vāpi pavitrayati mānavam ..3..
contentBlocks.translation:
Oh Tulsi, daily protect me,
Always from all dangers,
For you make than one holy,
Who sings or thinks about you.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलसी (tulasī) – Tulsi; पातु (pātu) – protect; माम् (mām) – me; नित्यम् (nityaṃ) – always; सर्वापद्भ्यः (sarvāpadbhyaḥ) – from all dangers (sarva:all | apadbhyah:from dangers); अपि (api) – even; सर्वदा (sarvadā) – always; कीर्तिता (kīrtitā) – praised; अपि (api) – even; स्मृता (smṛtā) – remembered; वा (vā) – or; अपि (api) – even; पवित्रयति (pavitrayati) – makes holy; मानवम् (mānavam) – human;

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि शिरसा देवीं तुलसीं विलसत्तनुम् ।
यां दृष्ट्वा पापिनो मर्त्या मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषात् ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi śirasā devīṃ tulasīṃ vilasattanum .
yāṃ dṛṣṭvā pāpino martyā mucyante sarvakilbiṣāt ..4..
contentBlocks.translation:
I prostrate with my head before the Goddess,
Who gives brightness to our body,
And seeing her, the sinful humans,
Would get rid of all sort of sins.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमामि (namāmi) – I prostrate; शिरसा (śirasā) – with my head; देवीं (devīṃ) – Goddess; तुलसीं (tulasīṃ) – Tulasī; विलसत्तनुम् (vilasattanum) – with shining body (vilasat:shining | tanum:body); यां (yāṃ) – whom; दृष्ट्वा (dṛṣṭvā) – seeing; पापिनः (pāpinaḥ) – sinful humans; मर्त्याः (martyāḥ) – mortals; मुच्यन्ते (mucyante) – are liberated; सर्वकिल्बिषात् (sarvakilbiṣāt) – from all sins (sarva:all | kilbiṣāt:from sins);

contentBlocks.original:
तुलस्या रक्षितं सर्वं जगदेतच्चराचरम् ।
या विनिहन्ति पापानि दृष्ट्वा वा पापिभिर्नरैः ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasyā rakṣitaṃ sarvaṃ jagadetaccarācaram .
yā vinihanti pāpāni dṛṣṭvā vā pāpibhirnaraiḥ ..5..
contentBlocks.translation:
Tulsi protects all,
The universe with its movable and immovable,
And when a man who is a sinner,
Sees it, it destroys all his sins.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलस्या (tulasyā) – by Tulsi; रक्षितं (rakṣitaṃ) – protected; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; जगत् (jagat) – universe; एतत् (etat) – this; चराचरम् (carācaram) – movable and immovable (cara:movable | acara:immovable); या (yā) – which; विनिहन्ति (vinihanti) – destroys; पापानि (pāpāni) – sins; दृष्ट्वा (dṛṣṭvā) – seeing; वा (vā) – or; पापिभिः (pāpibhiḥ) – by sinners; नरैः (naraiḥ) – by men;

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तुलस्यतितरां यस्यै बद्ध्वाञ्जलिं कलौ ।
कलयन्ति सुखं सर्वं स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथाऽपरे ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastulasyatitarāṃ yasyai baddhvāñjaliṃ kalau .
kalayanti sukhaṃ sarvaṃ striyo vaiśyāstathā'pare ..6..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to Tulsi the great,
Who removes the bondage of Kali age,
And it blesses with a pleasant life,
To women and the merchant class.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; तुलस्य (tulasya) – to Tulsi; अतितरां (atitarāṃ) – exceedingly; यस्यै (yasyai) – to whom; बद्ध्वा (baddhvā) – having joined; अञ्जलिं (añjaliṃ) – hands in reverence; कलौ (kalau) – in the Kali age; कलयन्ति (kalayanti) – consider; सुखं (sukhaṃ) – happiness; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; स्त्रियः (striyaḥ) – women; वैश्याः (vaiśyāḥ) – merchants; तथा (tathā) – and; अपरे (apare) – others;

contentBlocks.original:
तुलस्या नापरं किञ्चिद् दैवतं जगतीतले ।
यथा पवित्रितो लोको विष्णुसङ्गेन वैष्णवः ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasyā nāparaṃ kiñcid daivataṃ jagatītale .
yathā pavitrito loko viṣṇusaṅgena vaiṣṇavaḥ ..7..
contentBlocks.translation:
There is nothing in this world,
As divine as Tulsi,
For it makes the world pure,
And blesses Vaishnavas,
With company of Lord Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलस्या (tulasyā) – of Tulsi; न (na) – not; अपरं (aparaṃ) – other; किञ्चित् (kiñcit) – anything; दैवतं (daivataṃ) – divine; जगतीतले (jagatītale) – on the earth (jagati:earth | tale:on); यथा (yathā) – as; पवित्रितः (pavitritaḥ) – purified; लोकः (lokaḥ) – world; विष्णुसङ्गेन (viṣṇusaṅgena) – by company of Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | saṅgena:by company); वैष्णवः (vaiṣṇavaḥ) – Vaishnava;

contentBlocks.original:
तुलस्याः पल्लवं विष्णोः शिरस्यारोपितं कलौ ।
आरोपयति सर्वाणि श्रेयांसि वरमस्तके ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasyāḥ pallavaṃ viṣṇoḥ śirasyāropitaṃ kalau .
āropayati sarvāṇi śreyāṃsi varamastake ..8..
contentBlocks.translation:
In the kali age, If thulasi lea,
Is offered to the head of Vishnu,
It is same thing as receiving,
The blessings of Vishnu on your head.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलस्याः (tulasyāḥ) – of Tulasi; पल्लवं (pallavaṃ) – leaf; विष्णोः (viṣṇoḥ) – of Vishnu; शिरसि (śirasi) – on the head; आरोपितं (āropitaṃ) – placed; कलौ (kalau) – in the Kali age; आरोपयति (āropayati) – raises; सर्वाणि (sarvāṇi) – all; श्रेयांसि (śreyāṃsi) – blessings; वरमस्तके (varamastake) – on the best head (vara:best | mastake:on the head);

contentBlocks.original:
तुलस्यां सकला देवा वसन्ति सततं यतः ।
अतस्तामर्चयेल्लोके सर्वान् देवान् समर्चयन् ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasyāṃ sakalā devā vasanti satataṃ yataḥ .
atastāmarcayelloke sarvān devān samarcayan ..9..
contentBlocks.translation:
Always in the Tulsi plant,
All gods live forever,
And so worshipping Tulsi,
Is same as worshipping all devas.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलस्याम् (tulasyām) – in the Tulsi plant; सकला (sakalā) – all; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; वसन्ति (vasanti) – live; सततं (satataṃ) – always; यतः (yataḥ) – because; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; ताम् (tām) – her; अर्चयेत् (arcayet) – should worship; लोके (loke) – in the world; सर्वान् (sarvān) – all; देवान् (devān) – gods; समर्चयन् (samarcayan) – worshipping;

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तुलसि सर्वज्ञे पुरुषोत्तमवल्लभे ।
पाहि मां सर्वपापेभ्यः सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायिके ॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastulasi sarvajñe puruṣottamavallabhe .
pāhi māṃ sarvapāpebhyaḥ sarvasampatpradāyike ..10..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to all knowing Tulsi,
Who is the consort of Lord Vishnu,
Save me from all the sins.
Oh bestower of all sort of wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; तुलसि (tulasi) – Tulsi; सर्वज्ञे (sarvajñe) – all-knowing; पुरुषोत्तमवल्लभे (puruṣottamavallabhe) – consort of Lord Vishnu (puruṣottama:Lord Vishnu | vallabhe:consort of); पाहि (pāhi) – protect; मां (māṃ) – me; सर्वपापेभ्यः (sarvapāpebhyaḥ) – from all sins (sarva:all | pāpebhyaḥ:from sins); सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायिके (sarvasampatpradāyike) – bestower of all wealth (sarva:all | sampat:wealth | pradāyike:bestower of);

contentBlocks.original:
इति स्तोत्रं पुरा गीतं पुण्डरीकेण धीमता ।
विष्णुमर्चयता नित्यं शोभनैस्तुलसीदलैः ॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti stotraṃ purā gītaṃ puṇḍarīkeṇa dhīmatā .
viṣṇumarcayatā nityaṃ śobhanaistulasīdalaiḥ ..11..
contentBlocks.translation:
This prayer was composed,
By Pundareeka the able one,
Who worshipped lord Vishnu daily,
By the divine leaf of Tulsi.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; पुरा (purā) – formerly; गीतं (gītaṃ) – sung; पुण्डरीकेण (puṇḍarīkeṇa) – by Puṇḍarīka; धीमता (dhīmatā) – by the wise; विष्णुमर्चयता (viṣṇumarcayatā) – worshipping Vishnu (viṣṇum:Vishnu | arcayatā:worshipping); नित्यं (nityaṃ) – daily; शोभनैः (śobhanaiḥ) – auspicious; तुलसीदलैः (tulasīdalaiḥ) – with Tulsi leaves (tulasī:Tulsi | dalaiḥ:with leaves);

contentBlocks.original:
तुलसी श्रीर्महालक्ष्मीर्विद्याविद्या यशस्विनी ।
धर्म्या धर्मानना देवी देवीदेवमनःप्रिया ॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasī śrīrmahālakṣmīrvidyāvidyā yaśasvinī .
dharmyā dharmānanā devī devīdevamanaḥpriyā ..12..
contentBlocks.translation:
Oh Tulsi, Oh Mahalakshmi,
Oh wisdom, Oh ignorance, Oh famous one,
Oh Charity, oh Goddess, Oh she Who makes us give,
The daring of the mind of Lord Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलसी (tulasī) – Tulsi; श्रीः (śrīḥ) – Lakshmi; महालक्ष्मीः (mahālakṣmīḥ) – Mahalakshmi; विद्या (vidyā) – wisdom; अविद्या (avidyā) – ignorance; यशस्विनी (yaśasvinī) – famous one; धर्म्या (dharmyā) – Charity; धर्मानना (dharmānanā) – Goddess (dharma:righteousness | ānanā:face); देवी (devī) – Goddess; देवीदेवमनःप्रिया (devīdevamanaḥpriyā) – mind of Lord Vishnu (devī:Goddess | deva:Lord | manaḥ:mind | priyā:beloved);

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीप्रियसखी देवी द्यौर्भूमिरचला चला ।
षोडशैतानि नामानि तुलस्याः कीर्तयन्नरः ॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīpriyasakhī devī dyaurbhūmiracalā calā .
ṣoḍaśaitāni nāmāni tulasyāḥ kīrtayannaraḥ ..13..
contentBlocks.translation:
Oh Mahalakshmi, Oh goddess who is a dear friend,
Oh Goddess, she who is in the mind,
Oh earth, oh movable one, Oh immovable one,
If these sixteen names are sung by men.
contentBlocks.meanings: लक्ष्मीप्रियसखी (lakṣmīpriyasakhī) – dear friend of Lakshmi (lakṣmī:Lakshmi | priya:dear | sakhī:friend); देवी (devī) – goddess; द्यौः (dyaus) – sky; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; अचला (acalā) – immovable; चला (calā) – movable; षोडश (ṣoḍaśa) – sixteen; एतानि (etāni) – these; नामानि (nāmāni) – names; तुलस्याः (tulasyāḥ) – of Tulasi; कीर्तयन् (kīrtayan) – singing; नरः (naraḥ) – man;

contentBlocks.original:
लभते सुतरां भक्तिमन्ते विष्णुपदं लभेत् ।
तुलसी भूर्महालक्ष्मीः पद्मिनी श्रीर्हरिप्रिया ॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labhate sutarāṃ bhaktimante viṣṇupadaṃ labhet .
tulasī bhūrmahālakṣmīḥ padminī śrīrharipriyā ..14..
contentBlocks.translation:
They get incomparable devotion.
And at the end reach the place of Vishnu,
Oh Tulsi, Oh earth, Oh Maha Lakshmi,
Oh goddess of lotus, oh Darling of Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: लभते (labhate) – obtains; सुतरां (sutarāṃ) – incomparable; भक्तिम् (bhaktim) – devotion; अन्ते (ante) – at the end; विष्णुपदं (viṣṇupadam) – place of Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | padam:place); लभेत् (labhet) – reaches; तुलसी (tulasī) – Tulsi; भूः (bhūḥ) – earth; महालक्ष्मीः (mahālakṣmīḥ) – Maha Lakshmi (mahā:great | lakṣmīḥ:Lakshmi); पद्मिनी (padminī) – goddess of lotus; श्रीः (śrīḥ) – goddess of wealth; हरिप्रिया (haripriyā) – Darling of Vishnu (hari:Vishnu | priyā:darling);

contentBlocks.original:
तुलसि श्रीसखि शुभे पापहारिणि पुण्यदे ।
नमस्ते नारदनुते नारायणमनःप्रिये ॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tulasi śrīsakhi śubhe pāpahāriṇi puṇyade .
namaste nāradanute nārāyaṇamanaḥpriye ..15..
contentBlocks.translation:
Oh Tulsi, Oh friend of Lakshmi, Oh holy one.
Oh remover of sins, Oh one who blesses with divinity,
Salutations to you who was sung by sage Narada,
Oh darling of the mind of Lord Vishnu.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुलसि (tulasi) – Oh Tulsi; श्रीसखि (śrīsakhi) – Oh friend of Lakshmi (śrī:Lakshmi | sakhi:friend); शुभे (śubhe) – Oh holy one; पापहारिणि (pāpahāriṇi) – Oh remover of sins (pāpa:sins | hāriṇi:remover); पुण्यदे (puṇyade) – Oh one who blesses with divinity (puṇya:divinity | de:giver); नमस्ते (namaste) – Salutations to you; नारदनुते (nāradanute) – sung by sage Narada (nārada:sage Narada | nute:sung by); नारायणमनःप्रिये (nārāyaṇamanaḥpriye) – darling of the mind of Lord Vishnu (nārāyaṇa:Lord Vishnu | manaḥ:mind | priye:darling);

contentBlocks.original: इथि पुन्दरेएक क्रुथम् तुल्सि स्तोत्रम् सम्पोओर्नम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: Ithi Pundareeka krutham Tulsi stotram sampoornam
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the Tulsi Stotram composed by Sri Pundarika
contentBlocks.meanings: इथि (ithi) – thus; पुन्दरेएक (pundarīka) – Pundarika; क्रुथम् (kr̥tam) – composed; तुल्सि (tulasi) – Tulsi; स्तोत्रम् (stotram) – hymn; सम्पोओर्नम् (sampūrṇam) – complete;
